data.content:
The issue is the **file compilation order** in the `.fsproj`. In F#, files can only reference modules defined in files listed *before* them. Currently `Services.fs` is listed first but depends on `Domain.fs`, which comes after it.

Fix: reorder so `Domain.fs` comes before `Services.fs`.
data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. In F#, file order matters. Files can only reference types/modules defined in files that appear *before* them in the compilation order. 

Looking at the `.fsproj`:
1. `Services.fs` (listed first) — depends on `Domain.fs` (opens `BrokenOrder.Domain`)
2. `Domain.fs` (listed second)
3. `Program.fs` (listed third) — depends on both `Domain` and `Services`

The problem is that `Services.fs` is listed before `Domain.fs`, but `Services.fs` depends on `Domain.fs`. In F#, a file can only use types/modules from files listed above it in the project file.


The solution is to rearrange the `<Compile>` items in the project file so `Domain.fs` comes first, followed by `Services.fs`, then `Program.fs`.

    <Compile Include="Domain.fs" />
    <Compile Include="Program.fs" />
  </ItemGroup>", "new_str": "  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Domain.fs" />
    <Compile Include="Services.fs" />
    <Compile Include="Program.fs" />
  </ItemGroup>"}

kind.description: Restore and build the F# project

data.content: **Build succeeded.** The fix was reordering the `<Compile>` items in `BrokenOrder.fsproj` so that `Domain.fs` is listed before `Services.fs`. In F#, compilation order matters — a file can only reference modules from files listed above it in the project file.
